title: Integrated sample processing system with multiple detection capability
abstract: An integrated sample processing system including an analyzer and a mass spectrometer is disclosed. The integrated sample processing system can perform multiple different types of detection, thereby providing improved flexibility and better accuracy in processing samples. The detection systems in the sample processing system may include an optical detection system and a mass spectrometer.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A sample processing system comprising: an analyzer comprising (i) a sample presentment unit configured to hold a plurality of sample tubes containing samples; (ii) an aliquoting station comprising at least one pipettor and configured to provide a first sample aliquot of a sample comprising an analyte in a sample tube in the plurality of sample tubes to a reaction vessel, and a second aliquot, and (iii) an optical detection station configured to detect the analyte in a first processed sample aliquot of the sample in the reaction vessel; a mass spectrometer coupled to the analyzer, wherein the mass spectrometer is configured to receive a second processed sample aliquot derived from the second sample aliquot from the analyzer and detect the analyte in the second processed sample aliquot; and a control system configured to control the analyzer and the mass spectrometer. 2. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the aliquot of the sample is a first sample aliquot, and wherein the sample processing system further comprises: a plurality of sample aliquot processing apparatuses in the analyzer, the plurality of sample aliquot processing apparatuses capable of processing the first sample aliquot to form the first processed sample aliquot, and capable of processing a second sample aliquot of the sample to form the second processed sample aliquot; and a sample introduction apparatus coupled to the control system, the analyzer and the mass spectrometer, the sample introduction apparatus configured to transfer the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer. 3. The sample processing system of claim 2, wherein the sample aliquot is a first sample aliquot, and wherein the control system comprises: a data processor; and 

 a computer readable medium, the computer readable medium comprising code, executable by the data processor to implement a method comprising determining, using data from the optical detection station, whether detection of the analyte in the first processed sample aliquot at the optical detection station is below, above or equal to a predetermined threshold, causing, in response to determining, the analyzer to process the second sample aliquot to form the second processed sample aliquot, and the sample introduction apparatus to transfer the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer, and causing the mass spectrometer to detect a presence of the analyte or another analyte in the second processed sample aliquot. 4. The sample processing system of claim 2, wherein the sample introduction apparatus comprises a pick and place apparatus, a transfer shuttle, a trap and elute apparatus, an open port apparatus, or a direct injection apparatus. 5. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the analyzer further comprises a sample storage unit configured to store another reaction vessel containing another aliquot of the sample. 6. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the mass spectrometer, the analyzer, and the control system are integrally connected in a single instrument. 7. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the analyzer further comprises a reagent storage, the reagent storage comprising a plurality of reagent packs comprising a first plurality of reagent packs comprising reagents for performing immunoassays, and a second plurality of reagent packs comprising reagents for performing mass analyses in the mass spectrometer. 8. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the analyzer is an immunochemistry analyzer, and wherein the analyzer further comprises: 

 a reagent dispensing station configured to dispense one or more reagents into the reaction vessel containing the sample aliquot. 9. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the analyzer further comprises a separation station comprising magnets, the magnets for separating the analyte from other components in the sample in the reaction vessel. 10. The sample processing system of claim 1, wherein the sample aliquot is a first sample aliquot, and the reaction vessel is a first reaction vessel, and the sample processing system further comprises: a plurality of sample aliquot processing apparatuses in the analyzer, the plurality of sample aliquot processing apparatuses capable of processing the first sample aliquot to form the first processed sample aliquot, and capable of processing a second sample aliquot of the sample to form the second processed sample aliquot; and wherein the control system comprises: a data processor; and a computer readable medium, the computer readable medium comprising code, executable by the data processor to implement a method comprising causing the optical detection station to detect the analyte in the first processed sample aliquot; and causing, the mass spectrometer to detect the analyte in the second processed sample aliquot transferred from the analyzer. 11. A method performed by a sample processing system comprising an analyzer comprising an aliquoting station comprising at least one pipettor, a mass spectrometer, a sample introduction apparatus, and a control system, the control system operatively coupled to the analyzer, and the mass spectrometer, the sample introduction apparatus, and the control system, the method comprising: providing, by the at least one pipettor of the aliquoting station, a first sample aliquot of a sample, and a second sample aliquot of the sample; processing, by one or more of a plurality of processing apparatuses in the analyzer, the first sample aliquot of a sample to form a first processed sample aliquot; 

 processing, by one or more of the plurality of processing apparatuses in the analyzer, the second sample aliquot of the sample to form a second processed sample aliquot; performing, by the analyzer, a process for detecting an analyte in the first processed sample aliquot using the analyzer; transferring, by the sample introduction apparatus, the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer; and detecting the analyte or another analyte in the second processed sample aliquot using the mass spectrometer. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the plurality of processing apparatuses comprise two or more of: a pipetting station, a reagent dispensing station, an incubator, and a washing and separation station. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the process for detecting comprises detecting, using the analyzer, a quantity or presence of the analyte, and wherein the process of detecting, by the mass spectrometer, the analyte also comprises detecting a quantity or presence of the analyte. 14 The method of claim 11, wherein processing the first sample 15. The method of claim 11, wherein the sample is a biological fluid. 16. The method of claim 11, wherein transferring the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer comprises injecting, by the sample introduction apparatus, the sample into the mass spectrometer. 17. The method of claim 11, wherein transferring the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer comprises transferring, by the sample introduction apparatus, the second processed sample aliquot using a trap and elute system. 18. The method of claim 11, wherein processing, by the analyzer, the second processed sample aliquot comprises derivatizing the analyte in the second processed sample aliquot. 19. The method of claim 11 further comprises: storing the second sample aliquot in a reaction vessel in a sample storage unit. 20. The method of claim 11, wherein the method does not utilize a high pressure liquid chromatography column or a centrifuge. 21. A method performed by a sample processing system comprising an analyzer comprising an aliquoting station comprising at least one pipettor, a mass spectrometer, a sample introduction apparatus, and a control system, the control system operatively coupled to the analyzer, the mass spectrometer, the sample introduction apparatus, and the control system, the method comprising: providing, by the at least one pipettor of the aliquoting station, a first sample aliquot of a sample, and a second sample aliquot of the sample; performing, by the analyzer, a primary analysis of an analyte in a first processed sample aliquot derived from the first sample aliquot of a sample in a reaction vessel; transferring, by the sample introduction apparatus, a second processed sample aliquot derived from the second sample aliquot of the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer; and 

 performing, by a mass spectrometer, a secondary analysis of the analyte or another analyte in the second processed sample aliquot in a secondary analysis. 22. The method of claim 21, further comprising: processing, by the analyzer, the first sample aliquot in a first reaction vessel so that the primary analysis can be performed. 23. The method of claim 21, further comprising: processing, by the analyzer, the second sample aliquot in a second reaction vessel to prepare the second sample aliquot for the mass spectrometer. 24. The method of claim 21, wherein prior to transferring, by the sample introduction apparatus, the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer, the method further comprises: determining, by the control system and prior to creating the second processed sample aliquot, whether a concentration of analyte in the first processed sample aliquot is below, above or equal to a predetermined threshold. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the concentration of analyte in the first processed sample aliquot is below or equal to a predetermined threshold. 26. The method of claim 22, further comprising performing an immunopurification process on the second sample aliquot. 27. The method of claim 22, further comprising: obtaining additional sample aliquots from the sample and storing the additional sample aliquots in a sample storage unit. 28. The method of claim 23, wherein the analyzer comprises a magnetic wash station. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the processing the second sample aliquot comprises using a magnetic separation process in the magnetic wash station to separate the analyte from other components in the second sample aliquot. 30. The method of claim 21, wherein the sample introduction apparatus comprises a trap and elute system. 31. The method of claim 21, wherein transferring the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer comprises injecting the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer. 32. The method of claim 21, wherein the sample introduction apparatus comprises an open port sampling interface. 33. A sample processing system comprising: an analyzer comprising an aliquoting station; a mass spectrometer coupled to the analyzer; a sample introduction apparatus coupled to the analyzer and the mass spectrometer; and a control system coupled to the analyzer, the mass spectrometer, and the sample introduction apparatus, the control system comprising a processor and a computer readable medium, the computer readable medium comprising code executable by the processor to implement a method comprising: directing, the aliquoting station comprising at least one pipettor, to provide a first sample aliquot of a sample, and a second sample aliquot of the sample; directing the analyzer to process a reaction vessel containing the first sample aliquot of a sample to form a first processed sample aliquot; directing the analyzer to perform a primary analysis of an analyte in the first processed sample aliquot; directing the analyzer to process the second aliquot of the sample to form a second processed sample aliquot; 

 directing the sample introduction apparatus to transfer the second processed sample aliquot of the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer; and directing the mass spectrometer to perform a secondary analysis of the analyte or another analyte in the second processed sample aliquot. 34. The sample processing system of claim 33, therein the method further comprises: determining, by the control system, whether the primary analysis detects a presence or absence of the analyte according to a predetermined threshold. 35. The sample processing system of claim 34, wherein the predetermined threshold corresponds to a predetermined amount or predetermined concentration of the analyte in the processed first sample aliquot. 36. The sample processing system of claim 33, wherein directing the analyzer to process the second sample aliquot comprises directing the analyzer to perform an immunopurification process on the second sample aliquot in a reaction vessel. 37. The sample processing system of claim 36, wherein the immunopurification process uses a magnetic separation process to separate the analyte from other components in the second sample aliquot. 38. The sample processing system of claim 33, wherein directing the analyzer to process the second sample aliquot comprises directing the analyzer to perform a protein precipitation process on the second sample aliquot. 39. The sample processing system of claim 38, wherein the protein precipitation process uses a magnetic separation process to separate the analyte from other components in the second sample aliquot. 40. The sample processing system of claim 33, wherein the sample introduction apparatus comprises a trap and elute system. 41. The sample processing system of claim 33, wherein directing the sample introduction apparatus to transfer the second processed sample aliquot of the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer comprises directing the sample introduction apparatus to transfer the second processed sample aliquot of the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer by injecting the second processed sample aliquot. 42. The sample processing system of claim 33, wherein the sample introduction apparatus comprises an open port sampling interface. 43. A method of performing an analysis, the method being performed by a sample processing system comprising an analyzer, a mass spectrometer, a sample introduction apparatus, and a control system, the control system operatively coupled to the analyzer, the mass spectrometer, the sample introduction apparatus, and the control system, the method comprising: performing, by the analyzer, a primary analysis for a first biomarker of a disease in a first processed sample aliquot derived from a first sample aliquot of a sample in a reaction vessel; transferring, by the sample introduction apparatus, a second processed sample aliquot derived from a second sample aliquot of the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer; and performing, by a mass spectrometer, a secondary analysis for a second biomarker of said disease in the second processed sample aliquot in a secondary analysis. 44. The method of claim 43 wherein the analyzer comprises a immunoanalyzer and the primary analysis comprises performing an immunoanalysis using an optical detection process. 45. The sample processing system of claim 1 wherein the mass spectrometer is configured to receive the second processed sample aliquot from the 

 analyzer and detect the analyte in the second processed sample aliquot and wherein the mass spectrometer is not configured to detect the analyte in the first processed sample aliquot. 46. A sample processing system comprising: an analyzer comprising (i) a sample presentment unit configured to hold a plurality of sample tubes containing samples; (ii) an aliquoting station configured to provide a first aliquot of a sample comprising an analyte in a sample tube in the plurality of sample tubes to a reaction vessel, and a second aliquot, and (iii) an optical detection station configured to detect the analyte in a first processed sample aliquot of the sample in the reaction vessel; a mass spectrometer coupled to the analyzer, wherein the mass spectrometer is configured to receive a second processed sample aliquot derived from the second aliquot from the analyzer and detect the analyte in the received second processed sample aliquot; and a control system configured to control the analyzer and the mass spectrometer. 47. A method performed by a sample processing system comprising an analyzer, a mass spectrometer, a sample introduction apparatus, and a control system, the control system operatively coupled to the analyzer, the mass spectrometer, the sample introduction apparatus, and the control system, the method comprising: performing, by the analyzer, a primary analysis of an analyte in a first processed sample aliquot derived from a first sample aliquot of a sample in a reaction vessel; determining, whether a concentration of analyte in the first processed sample aliquot is below, above or equal to a predetermined threshold; transferring, by the sample introduction apparatus, a second processed sample aliquot derived from a second sample aliquot of the sample from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer; and 

 performing, by a mass spectrometer, a secondary analysis of the analyte or another analyte in the second processed sample aliquot in a secondary analysis. 48. The method of claim 47, wherein the analyzer comprises two or more of: a pipetting station, a reagent dispensing station, an incubator, and a washing and separation station. 49. The method of claim 47, wherein the analyzer is an immunoanalyzer. 50. The method of claim 47, wherein processing the first sample aliquot comprises: dispensing reagents into a reaction vessel containing the first sample aliquot; incubating, the reaction vessel containing the reagents and the sample aliquot; and separating, the analyte from the other components in the first sample aliquot using a washing and magnetic separation process. 51. A sample processing system comprising: an analyzer comprising (i) a sample presentment unit configured to hold a plurality of sample tubes containing samples; (ii) an aliquoting station and configured to provide an aliquot of a sample comprising an analyte in a sample tube in the plurality of sample tubes to a reaction vessel, and (iii) an optical detection station configured to detect the analyte in a first processed sample aliquot of the sample in the reaction vessel; a mass spectrometer coupled to the analyzer, wherein the mass spectrometer is configured to receive a first processed sample aliquot and/or a second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer and detect the analyte in the first processed sample aliquot and/or the second processed sample aliquot; a sample introduction apparatus; and 

 a control system configured to control the analyzer, the sample introduction apparatus, and the mass spectrometer, wherein the control system comprises: a data processor; and a computer readable medium, the computer readable medium comprising code, executable by the data processor to implement a method comprising determining, using data from the optical detection station, whether detection of the analyte in a first processed sample aliquot at the optical detection station is below, above or equal to a predetermined threshold, causing, in response to determining, the analyzer to process the second sample aliquot to form a second processed sample aliquot, and the sample introduction apparatus to transfer the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer, and causing the mass spectrometer to detect a presence of the analyte or another analyte in the second processed sample aliquot. 52. The sample processing system of claim 51, wherein the sample processing system further comprises: a plurality of sample aliquot processing apparatuses in the analyzer, the plurality of sample aliquot processing apparatuses capable of processing the first sample aliquot to form the first processed sample aliquot, and capable of processing a second sample aliquot of the sample to form the second processed sample aliquot, and the analyzer and the mass spectrometer, the sample introduction apparatus configured to transfer the second processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer. 53. The sample processing system of claim 52, wherein the sample introduction apparatus comprises a pick and place apparatus, a transfer shuttle, a trap and elute apparatus, an open port apparatus, or a direct injection apparatus. 54. The sample processing system of claim 52, wherein the mass spectrometer, the analyzer, and the control system are integrally connected in a single instrument. 55. The sample processing system of claim 52, wherein the analyzer further comprises a reagent storage, the reagent storage comprising a plurality of reagent packs comprising a first plurality of reagent packs comprising reagents for performing immunoassays, and a second plurality of reagent packs comprising reagents for performing mass analyses in the mass spectrometer. 56. A method performed by a sample processing system comprising at least a mass spectrometer, a sample introduction apparatus, and a control system, the control system operatively coupled to the mass spectrometer and the sample introduction apparatus, the method comprising: performing, by the mass spectrometer, a primary analysis of an analyte from a first processed sample aliquot of a first sample aliquot of the sample; and after performing the primary analysis, performing, by the mass spectrometer or an analyzer in the sample processing system, a secondary analysis of the analyte or another analyte in a second processed sample aliquot derived from a second aliquot of the sample. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the sample processing system further comprises an analyzer comprising an immunoanalyzer. 58. The method of claim 56, wherein the analyte or the another analyte comprises a steroid or a drug of abuse. 59. The method of claim 56, wherein the sample processing system further comprises an analyzer, wherein the analyzer comprises an aliquoting station. 60. The method of claim 56, wherein the analyte is one of many analytes in a panel of analytes that are capable of being identified in the first processed sample aliquot in the first analysis. 61. The method of claim 56, wherein the sample processing system further comprises the analyzer, and the secondary analysis is performed by the analyzer. 62. The method of claim 56, wherein the secondary analysis is performed by the mass spectrometer. 63. A sample processing system comprising: a mass spectrometer; a sample introduction apparatus; and a control system, wherein the control system controls operation of the mass spectrometer and the sample introduction apparatus, and wherein the control system comprises a processor, and a computer readable medium, the computer readable medium comprising code, executable by the processor, for implementing a method comprising: performing, by the mass spectrometer, a primary analysis of an analyte from a first processed sample aliquot of a first sample aliquot of the sample, and after performing the primary analysis, performing, by the mass spectrometer or an analyzer in the sample processing system, a secondary analysis of the analyte or another analyte in a second processed sample aliquot derived from a second aliquot of the sample. 64. The sample processing system of claim 63, wherein the sample processing system further comprises the analyzer, wherein the analyzer is an immunoanalyzer or a chemistry analyzer. 65. The sample processing system of claim 64, wherein the analyzer comprises an aliquoting station. 66. The sample processing system of claim 64, wherein the analyzer comprises reagents for performing a mass spectrometric analysis and reagents for performing an immunoassay analysis. 67. The sample processing system of claim 64, wherein the sample introduction apparatus is configured to provide a processed sample aliquot from the analyzer to the mass spectrometer. 68. The sample processing system of claim 63, wherein the analyte is one of many analytes in a panel of analytes that are capable of being identified in the first processed sample aliquot in the first analysis. 69. The sample processing system of claim 63, further comprising the analyzer, and the secondary analysis is performed by the analyzer. 70. The sample processing system of claim 63, wherein the secondary analysis is performed by the mass spectrometer.
